atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds the async fees crate constants surface: HTTP, LLM, DKMS, persistent-agent, long-running HTTP, sovereign-agent, image, audio, video, ZK, model-registry address, ABI offset, settlement selector, maximum dimension/duration, and shared size-limit constants, plus precompile address re-exports and compile-time assertions that bound error fees against minimum escrow constants.

destinations.evidence: Destination file preserves the full 443-line added constants module, including the same public constants, precompile address re-exports, default model registry address, ABI offsets, settlement selectors, maximum size/dimension/duration limits, and compile-time assertions.

Destination usages resolve through the preserved constants surface: HTTP fee calculation uses HTTP_EXECUTOR_BASE_FEE_WEI, LLM error handling uses LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI, async delivery error settlement uses DA_ERROR_PHASE2_FEE_WEI, wallet and payload code use HTTP_CALL_MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES, and pool/payload/verifier code use the shared settlement selectors and registry address.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The source diff introduces `constants.rs` as a new shared constants module, and the destination preserves the module's public values and re-exports behaviorally exactly. Because downstream async fee, wallet, payload, verifier, and pool code refer to these same exported constants, the pricing, size-limit, selector, address, and ABI-offset semantics are retained without drift.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run a package check plus async-fee regression tests that assert HTTP/LLM/error-delivery fees, selector bytes, model registry address, and max input/output limits remain equal to the constants in this module.
atoms.notes: This atom covers only `crates/ritual-async-fees/src/constants.rs`. `src/delivery.rs` begins at the next diff boundary and remains for file-0277.

summary: The async fees constants module is exact-port preserved in the destination.
